msPCA 0.4.1
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

    Fraction of Variance Explained

    fraction_variance_explained.Rd

    Computes the fraction of variance explained (variance explained normalized by the trace of the covariance/correlation matrix) by a set of PCs.

    fraction_variance_explained(C, U)

    Arguments

    C

    A matrix. The correlation or covariance matrix (p x p).

    U

    A matrix. The matrix containing the r PCs (p x r).

    Value

    A float.

    Examples

    TestMat <- cor(datasets::mtcars)
mspcares <- mspca(TestMat, 2, c(4,4))
#> ---- Iterative deflation algorithm for sparse PCA with multiple PCs ---
#> Dimension: 11
#> Number of PCs: 2
#> Sparsity pattern:  4 4
#>   Iteration |      Objective value |     Feasibility Violation |       Time
#>           1 |                0.649 |                  1.00e+00 |      0.000
#>          11 |                0.642 |                  7.33e-01 |      0.001
#>          21 |                0.632 |                  4.79e-01 |      0.002
#>          31 |                0.629 |                  4.34e-01 |      0.008
#>          35 |                0.604 |                  2.22e-16 |      0.009
fraction_variance_explained(TestMat, mspcares$x_best)
#> [1] 0.6043866
